---
title: "Rosemary Flavouring"
slug: rosemary-flavouring
category: "Additives & Functional"
category-slug: additives-functional
keywords: ["Rosemary", "Flavouring", "Herb", "Mediterranean", "Aromatic", "Extract", "FMCG"]
description: "Rosemary (*Rosmarinus officinalis*), a fragrant evergreen shrub native to the Mediterranean, is a relatively recent introduction to India, cultivated in cool..."
---

## History and Sourcing

[Rosemary](../spices-seasonings/rosemary.qmd) (*Rosmarinus officinalis*), a fragrant evergreen shrub native to the Mediterranean, is a relatively recent introduction to India, cultivated in cooler regions. Its name, from Latin 'ros marinus' ('dew of the sea'), reflects its origins. Rosemary flavouring captures the herb's distinctive piney, peppery, and woody essence through various extraction or synthesis methods.

## Home Kitchen Use

In Indian home kitchens, fresh or dried rosemary is primarily used in contemporary and fusion cuisine, rather than traditional dishes. It is popular in marinades for grilled meats and vegetables, roasted potatoes, breads, and infused oils, adding a sophisticated aromatic dimension to European and Middle Eastern-inspired preparations.

## Industrial Applications

Industrially, [Rosemary Flavouring](rosemary-flavouring.qmd) (E-FLAVOURING) is widely used in FMCG for consistent flavour delivery in snack seasonings, processed meats, sauces, soups, and ready-to-eat meals. [Rosemary](../spices-seasonings/rosemary.qmd) extract flavouring (F-EXTRACT) is a plant-derived, often more potent, natural flavouring, sometimes chosen for its inherent antioxidant properties and to meet 'natural flavour' claims.

## Distinctions and Common Confusion

Consumers often confuse the fresh or dried rosemary herb with '[Rosemary Flavouring](rosemary-flavouring.qmd).' The herb offers a fresh, nuanced aroma and texture, while the flavouring is a concentrated form for consistent taste, which can be natural or artificial. [Rosemary](../spices-seasonings/rosemary.qmd) extract flavouring is a specific, often more potent, natural flavouring, distinct from the whole herb but aiming to replicate its essence.
